title: Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention policies
description: Desired state for Microsoft Purview DLP policies consumed by scripts/Deploy-DLPPolicies.ps1. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/dlp-learn-about-dlp

name.description: Unique policy identifier. Maps to the -Name parameter of New-DlpCompliancePolicy. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancepolicy

description.description: Free-form policy description. Maps to -Comment on New-DlpCompliancePolicy.

mode.description: Policy enforcement mode. Simulation-first per project-plan guiding principle 3. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/dlp-test-dlp-policies

priority.description: Optional integer used to order policy match evaluation. Lower values win.

locations.description: Per-workload location buckets. The first 6 fields (exchange, sharePoint, oneDrive, teams, endpoint, powerBI) map to the primary -ExchangeLocation / -SharePointLocation / -OneDriveLocation / -TeamsLocation / -EndpointDlpLocation / -PowerBIDlpLocation cmdlet parameters. The remaining 12 fields map to per-workload exception / on-premises / third-party variants on New-DlpCompliancePolicy (added by #519; not currently used by any lab-tenant policy). The generic -Locations parameter (used by the Microsoft-shipped Copilot policy with Workload=Applications, Location=Copilot.M365) is modeled separately under 'genericLocations'. See ADR 0032.

genericLocations.description: Generic -Locations parameter entries (each carries Workload + Location + optional metadata + Inclusions/Exclusions). Modeled per ADR 0032 for workloads that Microsoft surfaces through the JSON -Locations parameter rather than per-workload location buckets (e.g. Microsoft 365 Copilot, Workload=Applications, Location=Copilot.M365). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancepolicy

enforcementPlanes.description: Maps to -EnforcementPlanes on New-DlpCompliancePolicy / Set-DlpCompliancePolicy. The Microsoft-shipped Copilot policy uses 'CopilotExperiences'. Dropping this on round-trip would change the enforcement target on apply, so the schema models it explicitly. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancepolicy

policyTemplateInfo.description: Microsoft policy-template identity. Defensive exporter-write / applier-skip per ADR 0032: the exporter writes this block so round-trips are stable, but Get-DlpPolicySplat NEVER emits it to New-/Set-DlpCompliancePolicy because mutating it could re-type the policy (e.g. flip a Microsoft-template policy to a user-authored one). Comparator also skips this field.

adaptiveScopes.description: Per-workload Microsoft Purview adaptive policy scope references (#520). Distinct from the static `locations:` buckets: adaptive scopes are first-class Purview resources keyed by name/GUID and authored via New-AdaptiveScope. Each scope is referenced here by name (required) with an optional GUID for round-trip fidelity; the apply path resolves name->GUID via Get-AdaptiveScope at runtime and throws if a referenced scope does not exist in the tenant. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/purview-adaptive-scopes

rules.description: Nested rule entries. Each rule maps to New-DlpComplianceRule. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notes.description: Free-form pass-through marker. Used by the reconciler exporter to flag tenant-only state that is not yet modeled declaratively (e.g. generic Locations parameter for Microsoft 365 Copilot). The apply path skips Create/Update against the tenant when a policy or rule carries only structural fields plus 'notes'.

locationBucket.description: Either the literal string `All` or an array of workload-specific identifiers.

name.description: Unique rule identifier within the policy.

notes.description: Free-form pass-through marker. Used by the reconciler exporter to flag tenant-only state that is not yet modeled declaratively (e.g. Microsoft-shipped default-template rules whose predicate body is carried in 'AdvancedRule' as a JSON tree of grouped SITs / trainable classifiers). The apply path skips Create/Update against the tenant when a rule carries only structural fields plus 'notes'.

priority.description: Optional integer; lower wins within the policy.

sensitiveInfoTypes.description: SIT references by GUID. GUIDs are sourced from data-plane/classifications/sit-catalog.yaml (issue #80).

sensitivityLabels.description: Sensitivity-label references by display name. Resolved via Get-Label (Wave 1 labels, issue #65).

advancedRule.description: Declarative representation of the Microsoft `AdvancedRule` JSON predicate (rules with IsAdvancedRule=True). Mutually exclusive with `sensitiveInfoTypes` and `sensitivityLabels`. See docs/adr/0031-dlp-advancedrule-yaml-shape.md for the wire-to-YAML transform and Microsoft-shipped wire wrappers reconstructed at apply time. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

blockAccess.description: Block the protected action (download, share, etc.) for matched content.

notifyUser.description: Policy-tip recipients. Tokens: SiteAdmin, LastModifier, Owner, <UPN>, <group UPN>.

generateIncidentReport.description: Recipients for an incident report email per match.

enforcePortalAccess.description: Maps to -EnforcePortalAccess on New-DlpComplianceRule. Microsoft default is True; controls whether the rule's protected actions apply to the Microsoft Purview portal browsing surface. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 1 (#521 slice B). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyEmailExchangeIncludeAttachment.description: Maps to -NotifyEmailExchangeIncludeAttachment on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls whether notification emails for Exchange-detected matches include the offending attachment. Microsoft default is True. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 1 (#521 slice B). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

reportSeverityLevel.description: Maps to -ReportSeverityLevel on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls the severity stamped on incident reports / alerts generated by this rule. Microsoft default is 'Low'. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 1 (#521 slice B). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

comment.description: Maps to -Comment on New-DlpComplianceRule. Free-form operator-set string describing the rule's intent; distinct from the policy-level 'description' (which maps to -Comment on the policy). 15/16 lab-tenant rules populated. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 2 (#521 slice C). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

accessScope.description: Maps to -AccessScope on New-DlpComplianceRule. Restricts the rule predicate to content shared in-organization, out-of-organization, or per-user. Populated on rules that gate behavior by sharing direction (e.g. credit-card-shared-externally rules). The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 2 (#521 slice C). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyEmailCustomText.description: Maps to -NotifyEmailCustomText on New-DlpComplianceRule. Operator-authored body text injected into the policy-tip notification email when the rule matches. Empty string is treated as unset (field omitted on round-trip). Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3a (#521 slice D). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyPolicyTipCustomText.description: Maps to -NotifyPolicyTipCustomText on New-DlpComplianceRule. Operator-authored text shown in the in-product policy-tip surface (Office, OneDrive, SharePoint, Endpoint, Teams) when the rule matches. Distinct from -NotifyEmailCustomText (the policy-tip email body). Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3a (#521 slice D). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyPolicyTipDisplayOption.description: Maps to -NotifyPolicyTipDisplayOption on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls how the policy-tip surface displays the match. Allowed values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin an enum because the allowlist is not published as a strongly-typed enum on the cmdlet's parameter metadata and the live service returns values beyond the originally-assumed set (observed on the lab tenant: 'Tip' and 'Dialog'; also 'NotifyOnly' / 'NotificationOnly'). Consistent with the sibling notifyUserType / notifyOverrideRequirements / notifyEmailOnedriveRemediationActions fields. The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3a (#521 slice D). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyUserType.description: Maps to -NotifyUserType on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls which user role receives the policy-tip notification (e.g. 'NotSet' = Microsoft default, 'LastModifier', 'Owner', 'SiteAdmin'). Allowed values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema intentionally does not pin an enum because the allowlist is not currently published as a strongly-typed enum on the cmdlet's parameter metadata. The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3b (#521 slice E). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyOverrideRequirements.description: Maps to -NotifyOverrideRequirements on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls what an end user must supply to override the policy tip (e.g. 'None' = Microsoft default, 'WithJustification', 'WithFalsePositive'). Allowed values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin an enum (see notifyUserType description). The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3b (#521 slice E). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyEmailOnedriveRemediationActions.description: Maps to -NotifyEmailOnedriveRemediationActions on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls the OneDrive-specific remediation action that the notification email links to (e.g. 'NotSet' = Microsoft default). Allowed values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin an enum (see notifyUserType description). The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3b (#521 slice E). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

notifyAllowOverride.description: Maps to -NotifyAllowOverride on New-DlpComplianceRule. Controls whether end users may override the policy tip and, if so, what they must supply (e.g. 'WithJustification', 'WithFalsePositive', or a comma-joined combination when multi-value). The cmdlet parameter is declared System.Object[] but Get-DlpComplianceRule returns the value as a comma-joined System.String; the schema and reconciler honor the wire shape (string). Allowed values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin an enum (see notifyUserType description). The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3c (#521 slice F). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

incidentReportContent.description: Maps to -IncidentReportContent on New-DlpComplianceRule. Comma-joined field set that the incident-report payload includes (e.g. 'Title, DocumentAuthor, DocumentLastModifier, Service, MatchedItem, RulesMatched, Detections, Severity, RetentionLabel, SensitivityLabel'). The cmdlet parameter is declared System.Object[] but Get-DlpComplianceRule returns the value as a comma-joined System.String; the schema and reconciler honor the wire shape (string). Allowed values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin an enum (see notifyUserType description). The 'None' / absent case is represented by omitting the field. Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 3c (#521 slice F). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

endpointDlpRestrictions.description: Maps to -EndpointDlpRestrictions on New-DlpComplianceRule. Per-app enforcement matrix for endpoint policies; each item is a {setting, value, appgroup, defaultmessage} object that controls one enforcement vector (e.g. setting=CloudEgress with value=Audit / Block / BlockWithJustification, scoped to an appgroup or 'none' for all apps). The cmdlet parameter is declared System.Object[] and the tenant returns ArrayList[Hashtable]; the reconciler normalizes by sorting items by `setting` so reorder-only diffs are silent. Allowed values for `setting` and `value` are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin enums (no published Learn-verified allowlist). Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 4/1 (#521 slice G). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

alertProperties.description: Maps to -AlertProperties on New-DlpComplianceRule. Free-form bag of per-rule alert-aggregation settings (open shape -- Microsoft may add or drop keys). The lab tenant currently populates this on the Microsoft-shipped 'Default DLP policy - Protect sensitive M365 Copilot interactions' rule with a single key {AggregationType: 'None'}. All values must be strings; the reconciler honors whatever the tenant returns. The Copilot routing decision is modeled separately via the policy-level `enforcementPlanes: CopilotExperiences` field (PR #515 era); per ADR 0033 §2 trigger-list evaluation, this field's actual shape does not require a new ADR. Allowed key names and values are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin enums (no published Learn-verified allowlist). Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 4/2 (#521 slice H). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

restrictAccess.description: Maps to -RestrictAccess on New-DlpComplianceRule. Array of per-action access restrictions on Microsoft 365 Copilot rules; each item is a {setting, value} object (e.g. setting=UploadText, value=Block). The cmdlet parameter is declared System.Object[] and the tenant returns ArrayList[Hashtable]; the reconciler normalizes by sorting items by `setting` so reorder-only diffs are silent. The umbrella #521 originally framed this as string[] but the live wire shape is a structured array identical to endpointDlpRestrictions (Batch 4/1). Per ADR 0033 §2 trigger-list evaluation (Copilot-crossover question), this field's actual shape does not require a new ADR -- the Copilot routing decision is modeled separately via policy-level `enforcementPlanes: CopilotExperiences`; this field parameterizes per-action enforcement on a Copilot rule. Allowed values for `setting` and `value` are validated server-side by the cmdlet at apply time; schema does not pin enums (no published Learn-verified allowlist). Tracked per ADR 0033 Batch 4/3 (#521 slice I). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/new-dlpcompliancerule

setting.description: Per-action restriction identifier on a Copilot rule. Example value observed in the lab tenant: UploadText. Server-side cmdlet validation is source of truth at apply time. Maps to the `setting` key on each -RestrictAccess item.

value.description: Enforcement action for this restriction. Example value observed in the lab tenant: Block. Server-side cmdlet validation is source of truth at apply time. Maps to the `value` key on each -RestrictAccess item.

setting.description: Endpoint enforcement vector identifier. Example values observed in the lab tenant: CloudEgress, CopyPaste, RemovableMedia, NetworkShare, UnallowedApps, Print, UnallowedBluetoothTransferApps, RemoteDesktopServices. Server-side cmdlet validation is source of truth at apply time. Maps to the `setting` key on each -EndpointDlpRestrictions item.

value.description: Enforcement action for this {setting, appgroup} pair. Microsoft default is 'Audit'; other documented values include 'Block', 'BlockWithJustification', 'Allow'. Server-side cmdlet validation is source of truth at apply time. Maps to the `value` key on each -EndpointDlpRestrictions item.

appgroup.description: App-group scope for the restriction. Microsoft default is the literal string 'none' (apply to all apps). Otherwise the human-readable name of an endpoint app-group authored under the Microsoft Purview portal. Maps to the `appgroup` key on each -EndpointDlpRestrictions item.

defaultmessage.description: Per-restriction policy-tip override. Microsoft default is the literal string 'none'. Maps to the `defaultmessage` key on each -EndpointDlpRestrictions item.

guid.description: GUID of the sensitive info type. Sourced from sit-catalog.yaml.

maxCount.description: Maximum match count. Microsoft's exported rules use -1 as the unbounded sentinel; absent or -1 means no upper bound.

displayName.description: Sensitivity label DisplayName as declared in data-plane/information-protection/labels.yaml.

outerOperator.description: Boolean operator joining the entries in `groups`. Maps to `Condition.SubConditions[0].Value[0].Operator` on the wire. See ADR 0031.

groups.description: Named SIT / trainable-classifier groups. Maps to `Condition.SubConditions[0].Value[0].Groups` on the wire.

name.description: Human-readable group label. Maps to `Groups[].Name` on the wire.

operator.description: Boolean operator joining the entries in `sensitiveInfoTypes` and `trainableClassifiers`. Maps to `Groups[].Operator` on the wire.

sensitiveInfoTypes.description: Built-in SIT entries. Each entry maps to one `Sensitivetypes[]` object on the wire (without `Classifiertype`).

trainableClassifiers.description: Trainable-classifier entries. Each entry maps to one `Sensitivetypes[]` object on the wire with `Classifiertype: MLModel`. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/trainable-classifiers-definitions

guid.description: GUID of the sensitive info type. Maps to `Sensitivetypes[].Id` on the wire.

name.description: Optional human-readable SIT name. Maps to `Sensitivetypes[].Name` on the wire; preserved for round-trip fidelity when Microsoft returns a non-GUID name.

maxCount.description: Maximum match count. Maps to `Maxcount`. Microsoft uses -1 as the unbounded sentinel.

confidenceLevel.description: SIT match confidence level. Maps to `Confidencelevel`.

guid.description: GUID of the trainable classifier. Maps to `Sensitivetypes[].Id` on the wire. Microsoft-shipped built-in classifiers are documented at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/trainable-classifiers-definitions; customer-trained models are tenant-resolvable by GUID.

name.description: Optional human-readable classifier name. Maps to `Sensitivetypes[].Name` on the wire; Microsoft typically returns the GUID as the name for trainable classifiers.

workload.description: Microsoft workload identifier. The Copilot policy uses 'Applications'. Maps to `Locations[].Workload` on the wire.

location.description: Workload-specific location identifier. The Copilot policy uses 'Copilot.M365'. Maps to `Locations[].Location` on the wire.

locationDisplayName.description: Optional human-readable location name returned by Microsoft. Round-tripped for fidelity; not required on apply. Maps to `Locations[].LocationDisplayName` on the wire.

locationSource.description: Microsoft-classified location source. Captured Copilot value is 'Unknown'. Round-tripped for fidelity. Maps to `Locations[].LocationSource` on the wire.

locationType.description: Microsoft-classified location type. Captured Copilot value is 'Unknown'. Round-tripped for fidelity. Maps to `Locations[].LocationType` on the wire.

inclusions.description: Tenants / groups / users the policy applies to. The Copilot policy uses a single Type=Tenant, Identity=All inclusion. Maps to `Locations[].Inclusions` on the wire.

exclusions.description: Tenants / groups / users excluded from the policy. Maps to `Locations[].Exclusions` on the wire.

type.description: Inclusion type, e.g. 'Tenant', 'Group', 'User'. Maps to `Inclusions[].Type` on the wire.

identity.description: Inclusion identity, e.g. 'All' for tenant-wide. Maps to `Inclusions[].Identity` on the wire.

displayName.description: Optional human-readable name. Round-tripped for fidelity. Maps to `Inclusions[].DisplayName` on the wire.

name.description: Optional Microsoft-internal name (typically equal to Identity for tenant-wide). Maps to `Inclusions[].Name` on the wire.

type.description: Exclusion type, e.g. 'Tenant', 'Group', 'User'. Maps to `Exclusions[].Type` on the wire.

identity.description: Exclusion identity. Maps to `Exclusions[].Identity` on the wire.

displayName.description: Optional human-readable name. Maps to `Exclusions[].DisplayName` on the wire.

name.description: Optional Microsoft-internal name. Maps to `Exclusions[].Name` on the wire.

policyTemplateInfo.description: Microsoft policy-template identity returned by Get-DlpCompliancePolicy. Defensive exporter-write / applier-skip per ADR 0032: structure is preserved verbatim from the wire so round-trips are byte-equal, but the apply path never emits this to the cmdlet. `additionalProperties: true` because Microsoft may evolve this shape; this field is treated as opaque pass-through.

name.description: Microsoft Purview adaptive policy scope name. Resolved via Get-AdaptiveScope at apply time; the cmdlet receives the scope name as the canonical input. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/purview-adaptive-scopes

guid.description: Optional adaptive scope GUID. Round-tripped from Get-DlpCompliancePolicy for traceability; the apply path resolves by name and treats the GUID as informational. Useful for detecting an adaptive scope rename across exports.
